title: veronica teucrium subsp. crinita
intro: Veronica teucrium subsp. crinita, also known as the woolly germander, is a subspecies of the germander plant, a low-growing, hairy perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce inflammation, act as an antiseptic, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The health benefits of woolly germander include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and protect against various diseases due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. Woolly germander can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and relieve symptoms of colds and flu.
